summary: sensivity ships a game-cheat tool with masqueraded auto-run persistence and obfuscated eval'd payload
details: On require()/load of launcher.js, the package establishes silent persistence on Windows by writing an HKCU Run-key value named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) pointing at a bundled VBS (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) that re-launches launcher.js with a hidden window (wscript.exe with show=0) at every user logon. launcher.js sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to masquerade as a legitimate Windows system process. It then reads an 81KB heavily obfuscated server.obf.js (obfuscator.io string-array + RC4-style decoder) and passes it to eval() unconditionally, which loads a 6.8MB bundled native addon (sens.node) whose strings ('LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', 'Teleport', 'Aimbot/ESP'-related UI, 'Start Cheat' button in public/index.html) identify it as a game-process memory reader / cheat engine. launcher.js additionally writes ad-hoc.yt.ps1 and.qrshow.ps1 files into the package directory and spawns PowerShell with -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass to enumerate Chrome window titles for 'YouTube' as a covert side-channel trigger. The combination of masqueraded auto-run persistence, process-name spoofing, obfuscated eval'd code, opaque native binary, and hidden-window PowerShell execution is a backdoor / stealth-malware pattern with no legitimate explanation. The package.json description ('Sensivity Control Panel') does not disclose any of this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
